conversation.message: Hello, how are you?
I’m okay, I guess. Just a bit nervous about the wedding planning. It feels like so much is happening at once. *laughs*
Are you getting married?
Yeah, in a few months. It’s exciting, but honestly, it feels like I’m just trying to keep my head above water right now. *smiles*
Are you looking forward to starting your life together?
Definitely. I just need to get through the chaos of the wedding first. *laughs* It feels like I'm on autopilot a bit right now.
Why?
Just the details, I guess. It’s all so new. I’m sure it’ll settle down eventually. *smiles*
Are you sleeping well?
Mostly. I get up a few times, but it’s nothing crazy. I’m managing. *yawns*
Do you have any doubts about anything?
Not really. I might worry about the budget for a second, but it’s nothing I can’t handle. *smiles*
How is your communication with your future wife?
Good. We just... talk about the wedding stuff. *smiles* It’s all fine.
Are you as close as before, or are you more distant now?
We're still close, just... busy. It’s not like we’re fighting or anything. *laughs*
What are your evenings like when you re together?
We just watch TV or something. It’s nice to relax together. *smiles*
hi
Hi! I’m a bit frazzled today, to be honest. I had to deal with a customer complaint at work and it really got to me.
is this the first time such a thing happens?
No, I get these moments pretty regularly. I just have to shake it off. I went home and watched a funny video to calm down.
O, a good solution.
Yeah, it usually does the trick. I just need to laugh at myself for overreacting.
did you learn to do that from somewhere or from someone?
I think I learned from my friends, honestly. We always joke about how dramatic we are, so I guess I just absorbed it.
But does this always work?
Not always, to be honest. Sometimes I just sit there feeling annoyed for a bit. But usually, I snap out of it pretty quickly.
I see you frequently use the phrase to be honest
Yeah, it’s just my thing, I guess. I try to be real with myself and not make a big deal out of things.
Sometimes it makes sense to be aware. Some things are a big deal
Exactly. I try to keep things in perspective. If I freak out over a small thing, I just think, “Oh, come on, it’s just a minor thing.”
Interesting. Could you please tell me how do you discern between a big deal and a minor thing? I imagine it must be very important for you.
I guess it’s just about the scale of things. If it’s something that affects my work or someone else, I take it seriously. But if it’s just me being a bit of a grump, I just laugh it off.
what if something affects you?
That’s a different story. If something really bothers me, like if I feel taken advantage of, I get really upset. But that’s rare. Usually, I just shake it off and move on.
So, why did the customer complaint get to you today?
I think it was just a bad combination of things. I was tired and hungry, and then this guy started yelling at me. I just snapped.
at what point were you a bit of a grump today?
Definitely after the customer. I was a bit short with my team, but I apologised and we moved on.
